metadata.summary: Function calling, Pydantic structured extraction via parse(), parallel tools, multimodal extensions (DALL-E, Whisper, TTS). API-level schema enforcement.

content.title: OpenAI Function Calling and Structured Outputs
content.summary: OpenAI-specific patterns for function calling (tool use), Pydantic-validated structured extraction via `client.beta.chat.completions.parse`, parallel tool calls, and multimodal extensions (DALL-E 3, Whisper, TTS). The primary distinction from generic tool use: structured outputs enforce schema compliance at the API level, not just via prompt instruction.

why.p: `client.beta.chat.completions.parse` with a Pydantic model enforces field presence at the API level — the model cannot produce a response missing required fields. This eliminates the most common class of extraction failures (missing or mistyped fields) that `json_object` mode cannot prevent.

list.item: Reliable, schema-validated JSON extraction from unstructured text
Pipeline driving external actions (API calls, DB writes) triggered by model decisions
Multiple tools needed in a single model response (parallel tool calls) to reduce round-trips
Image generation (DALL-E 3), speech-to-text (Whisper), or TTS alongside text LLM calls
Strict output format enforcement where `json_object` mode alone is insufficient
Only need a JSON blob without schema strictness — `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` is simpler
Simple prompting + regex post-processing is sufficient
Schema is deeply nested (>10 params) causing frequent misselection — simplify the schema first
Real-time audio generation at sub-200ms latency — TTS streaming is not suitable

section.name: openai-function-calling-loop-and-parallel-calls
section.p: How to define tools, run the request-execute-respond loop, and handle parallel tool calls safely with the OpenAI SDK.

rule.statement: Append the assistant message (with tool_calls) to messages before appending tool results; sort tool results by tool_call_id before the next API call.
rule.rationale: The OpenAI API requires the assistant message with tool_calls to appear before any tool result messages. Result order matters for the model's next reasoning step.

example.code:
import json from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
messages = [{"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in Kyiv?"}] response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=messages, tools=tools)
message = response.choices[0].message messages.append(message) # must append before tool results
if message.tool_calls: for tc in message.tool_calls: args = json.loads(tc.function.arguments) result = tool_registry[tc.function.name](**args) messages.append({ "role": "tool", "tool_call_id": tc.id, "content": json.dumps(result), }) final = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=messages, tools=tools) print(final.choices[0].message.content)

rule.statement: Validate tool call coherence before executing parallel calls — the model may batch contradictory calls (create and delete the same resource).
rule.rationale: OpenAI enables parallel tool calls by default. Without coherence checking, contradictory parallel calls can corrupt state before either result is returned to the model.

rule.statement: Use tool_choice="required" to force at least one tool call; use {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "..."}} to force a specific tool.
rule.rationale: tool_choice="auto" (default) lets the model skip tool use entirely when it believes an in-context answer is sufficient. Use "required" when the pipeline depends on a tool result.

section.name: pydantic-structured-extraction
section.p: Using client.beta.chat.completions.parse for schema-enforced structured output, JSON mode fallback, and critical gotchas (refusal, model support, null-check).

section.name: structured-output-rules

rule.statement: Use client.beta.chat.completions.parse with a Pydantic model for any extraction with required fields; fall back to json_object mode + manual validation only for models that do not support structured outputs (pre-gpt-4o).
rule.rationale: parse enforces field presence at the API level. json_object mode only prevents non-JSON responses; it does not enforce schema shape. Missing required fields cause downstream KeyErrors.

rule.statement: Always null-check response.choices[0].message.parsed before accessing fields — it is None if the model refused to fill the schema (e.g., safety filter triggered).
rule.rationale: A non-null message.refusal alongside a null parsed indicates a safety refusal. Accessing parsed without null-checking raises AttributeError in production.

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel from typing import Optional, List from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
class Person(BaseModel): name: str age: int email: Optional[str] = None
response = client.beta.chat.completions.parse( model="gpt-4o", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract: John Doe, 30, john@example.com"}], response_format=Person, )
msg = response.choices[0].message if msg.refusal: raise ValueError(f"Model refused: {msg.refusal}") person = msg.parsed # validated Pydantic instance print(person.name, person.age)

antipattern.description: Calling client.beta.chat.completions.parse on gpt-3.5-turbo or older models.
antipattern.reason: Structured Outputs are only available on gpt-4o and newer. Calling parse on unsupported models raises NotImplementedError at runtime.

section.reference: Full extraction with retry and fallback

section.name: dall-e-whisper-and-tts-rules
section.p: Critical rules for DALL-E 3 URL expiry, Whisper file size limits and chunking, and TTS model stability in agent pipelines.

rule.statement: Download and persist DALL-E 3 generated images immediately — URLs expire after 1 hour and cannot be regenerated.
rule.rationale: Agents that store the URL instead of the image content will have broken links in storage. This is the most common DALL-E 3 failure point in production pipelines.

rule.statement: Log both the original prompt and revised_prompt for every DALL-E 3 generation; the model rewrites prompts and may introduce content not in the original.
rule.rationale: DALL-E 3 automatically rewrites prompts via revised_prompt. For brand content or compliance, both versions must be reviewable. Agents generating images for brand content must log both.

example.code:
import requests from pathlib import Path from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
def generate_and_save(prompt: str, save_path: str) -> str: resp = client.images.generate( model="dall-e-3", prompt=prompt, size="1024x1024", quality="hd", response_format="url") data = resp.data[0] # Download immediately — URL expires in 1 hour img_bytes = requests.get(data.url).content Path(save_path).write_bytes(img_bytes) return data.revised_prompt # log for audit

section.name: whisper

rule.statement: Split audio files larger than 20MB into chunks with ffmpeg before sending to Whisper; repeat the prompt hint on each chunk to maintain proper noun consistency.
rule.rationale: Whisper has a 25MB hard limit. Agents processing long audio must chunk with overlap. The prompt parameter (proper noun hints) only affects the first segment unless repeated per chunk.

section.reference: Chunked transcription for large audio files

rule.statement: Pin TTS model to either tts-1 or tts-1-hd consistently within a conversation session — switching models changes voice characteristics noticeably and breaks user experience.
rule.rationale: tts-1 and tts-1-hd produce different voice quality profiles. Mid-conversation model switching is audibly jarring.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A subagent using OpenAI function calling should: define Pydantic models for structured extraction, use `client.beta.chat.completions.parse` for data extraction tasks, and use the standard tool-calling loop for action-oriented tasks. For multi-modal pipelines (text → image → TTS), the subagent should pipeline DALL-E and TTS calls asynchronously after the text generation completes to minimize total latency. Whisper transcription should be wrapped with automatic chunking for files >20MB.

list.item: faion-sdd-executor-agent — implement function schema registry, parallel tool execution, and Pydantic extraction pipeline as SDD tasks
General-purpose subagent — use structured output parsing to extract typed data from documents, emails, or database records

example.title: Extraction with Pydantic model
example.code: Extract the following fields from the text using the Person Pydantic model. Required fields: name, age, email. Optional: addresses. If a field is missing, use None. Return the parsed object. Text: {text}

example.title: Parallel tool execution

list.item: Use `client.beta.chat.completions.parse` (Pydantic structured output) over `json_object` mode for any schema with required fields; it enforces field presence at the API level
For DALL-E 3: always download and store the image within 60 minutes of generation; URL expiry is a frequent agent failure point
Specify `timestamp_granularities=["word"]` in Whisper only when you need word-level alignment; it doubles response size and latency for normal transcription
When using parallel tool calls, sort tool results by `tool_call_id` before appending to messages — the order matters for the model's next reasoning step
Pin DALL-E model to `dall-e-3` explicitly; `dall-e-2` is still the default in some SDK versions and produces lower quality output
`client.beta.chat.completions.parse` (Pydantic structured output) is only available on `gpt-4o` and `gpt-4o-mini`; calling it on `gpt-3.5-turbo` raises `NotImplementedError`
DALL-E 3 rewrites prompts via `revised_prompt`; the model may significantly change the intended composition — log `revised_prompt` and compare to original
DALL-E 3 image URLs expire after 1 hour; agents that store URLs instead of downloading and saving images immediately will have broken links in storage
Parallel tool calls: the model may batch two contradictory tool calls (e.g., create and delete the same resource); agents must validate tool call coherence before executing
Whisper has a 25MB file size limit; agents processing long audio must chunk the file with overlap to avoid transcript discontinuities at chunk boundaries
`response.choices[0].message.parsed` is `None` if the model refused to fill the schema (e.g., safety filter); check `message.refusal` before accessing `parsed`
DALL-E 3 `revised_prompt` can introduce content that was not in the original prompt; agents generating images for brand content must log both original and revised prompts for review
TTS voice characteristics vary significantly by model (`tts-1` vs. `tts-1-hd`); do not switch models mid-conversation — the voice changes noticeably and breaks user experience
Whisper `prompt` parameter (hints for proper nouns) affects only the first segment in chunked transcription; repeat the prompt for each chunk to maintain consistency

Human checkpoint before using function calling to write to production databases or send messages; the model selects which function to call based on natural language intent, which can be ambiguous — always show the user what action will be taken before executing

template.purpose: Structured output extraction with Pydantic + parse()
Large audio transcription via ffmpeg chunking (≤30 lines)
